channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Center Launches K-12 Student Video Contest to Celebrate America’s 250th Birthday #America250RI
item.description:
In recognition of President's Day and George Washington's upcoming birthday, the RI Center for Freedom & Prosperity today launched a video contest encouraging K-12 students to create a video about why they are proud to celebrate America's semiquincentennial, commemorating the 250th Anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

The post Center Launches K-12 Student Video Contest to Celebrate America’s 250th Birthday #America250RI appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture
item.description:
West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture Debates over hospital policy in West Virginia usually follow a familiar pattern. Hospital systems warn that their finances are weak. Industry groups say they have too many patients with government insurance. Certificate of Need (CON) rules are defended as needed to keep […]

The post West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] We Can’t Afford to Trim Your Trees, but We Can Hire Lobbyists
item.description: We’ve long called for reform to the practice of taxpayer-funded lobbying. But this NES response is probably the most glaring example of the need for reform.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Introducing Mississippi Momentum: A Blueprint for Lasting Prosperity
item.description:
Too many young people still leave Mississippi to chase opportunities elsewhere.  MCPP is on a mission to help change that - by creating the conditions for real, sustained growth so our children and grandchildren choose to stay, build lives, and thrive right here in our state. The good news?  Mississippi is no longer a laggard, but leading. […]

The post Introducing Mississippi Momentum: A Blueprint for Lasting Prosperity appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Ending the Sales Tax on Electricity
item.description:
Maine lawmakers are currently considering a proposal that would exempt all residential electricity from the state sales tax. While it may be a relatively small cut among all of Maine’s over-taxation and regulation, it is nevertheless good policy as well as an important acknowledgment of the growing reality that electricity costs are putting real pressure […]

The post Ending the Sales Tax on Electricity appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF Freedom Index Results Finalized
item.description:
As we have done for more than a decade, the Rio Grande Foundation has tracked the votes in New Mexico’s legislative sessions. The 2026 session was a short, 30-day legislative session, but some momentous bills passed (for better AND worse) and some horrible bills that could have passed were defeated, but often included floor votes […]

The post RGF Freedom Index Results Finalized appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Libertas] Three New Board Members Join Libertas Network
item.description:
Libertas Network is growing. As we expand our national programs to serve millions of families and spread the ideas of human flourishing, we need leaders who match the scale of that ambition. That’s why we’re excited to announce the addition of three extraordinary individuals to our board of trustees. Each one brings deep experience, bold […]

The post Three New Board Members Join Libertas Network appeared first on Libertas Network: Changing Hearts, Minds, and Laws.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Beyond the Fiscal Time Bomb: 5 Ways New Jersey Can Cut the Bloat and Empower Families This Year
item.description:
by Erica Jedynak, NJPI Board Member New Jersey is currently sitting on a fiscal time bomb. Despite record-shattering $58 billion […]

The post Beyond the Fiscal Time Bomb: 5 Ways New Jersey Can Cut the Bloat and Empower Families This Year first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] SOLAR, WIND & ARKANSAS MOTOR VEHICLES
item.description: (February 2026) Arkansans are heavily dependent on private transportation. The Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTS) reports that 93% of Arkansans reach work by private motor vehicles, while only 0.4% use public transportation. The 102,622 miles of roads in Arkansas dwarf the 2,505 miles of freight railroad and 1,860 miles of waterway cited by the BTS, […]

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: Strengthening Private Support for Mississippi Families
item.description:
This week at the Capitol has been quieter on major headline legislation, but that doesn’t mean meaningful policy conversations aren’t happening. One bill worth highlighting right now is HB 1944 — a proposal that focuses on strengthening private support for Mississippi families and nonprofit organizations serving vulnerable communities. Here’s what it does — and why […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: Strengthening Private Support for Mississippi Families appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] $45 Million, No Answers: NJEA Leadership Still Owes Teachers the Truth
item.description:
by Andy Mulvihill, NJPI Board Chairman How would you feel if you joined a union and paid $1,400 in dues […]

The post $45 Million, No Answers: NJEA Leadership Still Owes Teachers the Truth first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Will PPS Refuse to Comply with Federal Civil Rights Law?
item.description:
By John A. Charles, Jr. The United States Department of Education announced on...

The post Will PPS Refuse to Comply with Federal Civil Rights Law? appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraska Cigarette Tax Increase Would Raise Prices, Reduce Revenue Reliability, and Increase Smuggling
item.description:
*On March 4th, LB 1124, the bill to advance the tobacco tax increase failed on a bipartisan procedural vote with the following senators blocking the advancement: Sen. Andersen, Sen. Ballard, Sen. John Cavanaugh, Sen. Conrad, Sen. Guereca, Sen. Juarez, Sen. McKinney, Sen. Rountree, Sen. Storm, and Sen. Strommen. The Platte Institute is sympathetic in the...

The post Nebraska Cigarette Tax Increase Would Raise Prices, Reduce Revenue Reliability, and Increase Smuggling appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Mass. Officials Talk a Big Game About Democracy. Do They Practice What They Preach?
item.description:
Elected officials in Massachusetts sure have a lot to say about “democracy” these days.   For those paying attention to goings on around here in recent years, hearing politicians trumpeting their state as a citadel defending democracy and the rule of law, you might be...

The post Mass. Officials Talk a Big Game About Democracy. Do They Practice What They Preach?  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] LB 663: Nebraska Agricultural Zoning Reform Improves County Permitting Process
item.description:
On February 25, 2026, Governor Jim Pillen signed Legislative Bill 663 into law while at the Nebraska Dairy Convention and Trade Show in West Point, Nebraska. Introduced by Senator Tanya Storer, and championed by The Platte Institute, LB 663 improves county zoning processes for agricultural producers by establishing a more uniform, transparent, and timely system...

The post LB 663: Nebraska Agricultural Zoning Reform Improves County Permitting Process appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Empowerms] Dr. Clay Routledge: Meaning, Purpose, and Flourishing
item.description:
Dr. Clay Routledge: Meaning, Purpose, and Flourishing In this episode of The Empower Podcast, host Grant Callen sits down with Dr. Clay Routledge, Executive Vice President of the Archbridge Institute and Director of the Human Flourishing Lab, to explore what really makes life meaningful and why that matters for public policy in Mississippi and beyond.…

The post Dr. Clay Routledge: Meaning, Purpose, and Flourishing first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Emails Conflict with Health Commissioner’s Testimony on CDPAP
item.description:
The company selected to manage an $11 billion Medicaid home-care program discussed the job in detail with top Health Department....

The post Emails Conflict with Health Commissioner’s Testimony on CDPAP appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Beacontn] Why Tennessee Doesn’t Need a State Property Tax
item.description: We’ve made it just fine for more than 75 years without a state property tax, and we’ll be just fine in the future without one.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: RGF opinion piece: Medical malpractice success a win, but more needed
item.description:
The following appeared in the Albuquerque Journal on March 2nd, 2026 and in other newspapers across New Mexico. The passage of medical malpractice reform is the most important public policy success in New Mexico in more than a decade. It took support from New Mexicans of every political stripe and a bipartisan coalition in the Legislature to […]

The post Opinion piece: RGF opinion piece: Medical malpractice success a win, but more needed appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama Needs to Cut the Red Tape for Rural Hospitals
item.description:
Alabama’s rural hospitals are struggling, and our state’s bureaucratic red tape isn’t helping. The Alabama Hospital Association reports 27 hospitals in danger of closing and 19 in immediate risk.

The post Alabama Needs to Cut the Red Tape for Rural Hospitals appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama’s most influential political groups converge as ‘Freedom First Alabama’ to wage war on big government policies
item.description:
One of the most powerful political coalitions ever assembled in state history was launched today, bringing the state’s most influential industry and advocacy groups together with a singular purpose: to go on offense against policies that will raise costs for families and employers in Alabama.

The post Alabama’s most influential political groups converge as ‘Freedom First Alabama’ to wage war on big government policies appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] Choice and Charter Schools Outperform Public Schools, New Report Shows
item.description:
Students attending Wisconsin’s private choice and charter schools outperform their peers in traditional public schools in both English Language Arts (ELA) and math, even after accounting for demographic differences, according to a new Apples to Apples report released by WILL.

The post Choice and Charter Schools Outperform Public Schools, New Report Shows appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Why Kansas Wins When SCOTUS Stops Tariff Overreach
item.description: Kansas cannot treat trade as a talking point. Trade is a pillar of the state’s economy, from Wichita manufacturing to rural agriculture. The state’s Kansas International Trade Summary shows how closely Kansas jobs and incomes are tied to global markets, and the Kansas state profile from the U.S. Trade Representative highlights the scale of exports […]

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Idaho House Passes Freedom Foundation-Backed Bill Ending Taxpayer Support for Teachers Unions
item.description:
The Idaho House of Representatives has passed legislation backed by the Freedom Foundation that would end taxpayer support for teachers unions.

The post Idaho House Passes Freedom Foundation-Backed Bill Ending Taxpayer Support for Teachers Unions   appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: A Key Legislative Deadline — and a Bill to Help Mississippi’s Independent Workers
item.description:
Yesterday marked one of the most important procedural deadlines of the legislative session. It was the deadline for committees to report general bills and constitutional amendments originating in the other chamber. While it may sound technical, this deadline has a very real impact on which policies still have a chance of becoming law this year. Here’s […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: A Key Legislative Deadline — and a Bill to Help Mississippi’s Independent Workers appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Empirecenter] New York’s Residents Hit with Record Electricity Prices
item.description:
New York has some of the highest household energy prices in the nation, according to Empire Center and U.S. Energy....

The post New York’s Residents Hit with Record Electricity Prices appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Stephanie Smith: The $100 million myth vs. Alabama’s $12 billion reality
item.description:
Last week, during a "State of the Schools" event in North Alabama, leaders from Huntsville City, Madison City, and Madison County schools sounded a familiar but mathematically creative alarm.

The post Stephanie Smith: The $100 million myth vs. Alabama’s $12 billion reality appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Florida Politics: Cranking It to 11
item.description:
Study highlights strong academic gains tied to Florida scholarship programs Hello, Cleveland! A new study by scholar Patrick Graff of...

The post Florida Politics: Cranking It to 11 appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Teachers in flight
item.description:
Wisconsin’s public school teachers are leaving their classrooms at higher rates than they have in more than 25 years. Here, we present their voices.

The post Teachers in flight appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Who do unions really represent?
item.description:
Wherever our canvassing teams go, workers relate the same story: Not only are unions not informing members of their constitutional rights, but they’re being actively suppressed. 

The post Who do unions really represent?  appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Praises Department of Education for Title IX Investigation into New Richmond Schools, Encourages Further Action in Westosha and Sun Prairie
item.description:
WILL has issued a statement in response to the Department of Education's announcement of a Title IX investigation into the New Richmond School District.

The post WILL Praises Department of Education for Title IX Investigation into New Richmond Schools, Encourages Further Action in Westosha and Sun Prairie appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Failed Senate Bill 1511 Would Raise Death Tax to 20 Percent: It’s Time for IP51
item.description:
By John A. Charles, Jr. When Governor Kotek announced her “Prosperity Roadmap” in...

The post Failed Senate Bill 1511 Would Raise Death Tax to 20 Percent: It’s Time for IP51 appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 9
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Week 9 With only 30 days remaining in the 2026 legislative session, we cover some of the hottest topics at the Capitol: NIL, teacher pay, and school choice. 

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 9 first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Supreme Court delivers major win for California parents in Mirabelli case
item.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court this week delivered a decisive win for California parents and parental rights nationwide. In a 6-3 ruling, the High Court partially vacated the stay put in place by the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals in the high-profile Mirabelli case. In December, a federal district court in Southern California issued a statewide permanent injunction in Mirabelli...

The post Supreme Court delivers major win for California parents in Mirabelli case first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] SEIU 503 Mobilizes Members for Anti-ICE Protests—Using Union Resources to Advance a Political Agenda
item.description:
SEIU Local 503 says its primary mission is representing Oregon’s public-sector workers — caregivers, state employees and higher education staff. But recent events in Eugene highlight how the union increasingly mobilizes members not just for workplace representation, but for organized political activism tied to national ideological causes.

The post SEIU 503 Mobilizes Members for Anti-ICE Protests—Using Union Resources to Advance a Political Agenda appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Accessory Dwelling Units Accounted for At Least 10 Percent of New Homes Permitted in Massachusetts in 2025
item.description:
This month’s edition of The House Call discusses results from a state survey of municipalities on accessory dwelling unit permitting activity. It also describes the debate over whether building codes should be reformed to allow some apartment buildings to contain only one staircase.

The post Accessory Dwelling Units Accounted for At Least 10 Percent of New Homes Permitted in Massachusetts in 2025 appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Building Affordability: The Role of Single-Family Rentals in Expanding Housing Supply
item.description:
The housing affordability crisis is an issue being faced throughout the country.1This has caused frustration for hardworking Americans,...

The post Building Affordability: The Role of Single-Family Rentals in Expanding Housing Supply  appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] ‘Executive compensation’ regulation would hurt North Country hospitals
item.description:
New Hampshire has been lucky. Since 2005, 110 rural hospitals in the United States have closed, and another 85 have converted to an out-patient service provider. Maine was home to three of those conversions and one closure, all since 2013.  No closure or conversion has hit New Hampshire. Yet.  That could change if the state […]

The post ‘Executive compensation’ regulation would hurt North Country hospitals appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Bipartisan effort to kill property tax relief based on false claims, innuendo, and allegiance to local government
item.description: Virtually every member of the Kansas Legislature says they support property tax relief, but there is a bipartisan effort behind the scenes to kill it. The reasons vary, and some are unspoken (allegiance to local government, the proposed legislation isn’t their idea, etc.), and the objections are mostly based on false or unproven claims. The […]

item.title: [Will Law] Racial Achievement Gaps in Wisconsin Explained by Poverty and Family Structure, New Report Shows
item.description:
A new report from WILL examines why Wisconsin has some of the largest academic achievement gaps between Black and white students in the nation—and finds that poverty, family structure, and early literacy explain much of the disparity.

The post Racial Achievement Gaps in Wisconsin Explained by Poverty and Family Structure, New Report Shows appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Simple charts can empower and inform local voters
item.description:
New Hampshire’s cherished tradition of self-government has become eroded by the ever-increasing complexity of modern government. It is eroded further by the practice of sharing only very limited information with voters before and during local elections. A house bill this session would arrest this erosion by empowering voters with detailed but easy-to-understand information about their […]

The post Simple charts can empower and inform local voters appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Releases School Referenda Transparency Tool for Taxpayers
item.description:
IRG Releases School Referenda Transparency Tool for Taxpayers New resource helps Wisconsin voters understand local school referenda Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) released an online school referendum transparency tool on Tuesday to help taxpayers better understand school spending ahead of spring 2026 school district referenda decisions. The tool is available on […]

The post IRG Releases School Referenda Transparency Tool for Taxpayers appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Fewer Students, Greater Demand: How Regionalization Can Strengthen Massachusetts Schools
item.description:
Massachusetts faces a growing challenge: K-12 public school enrollment is declining, while the state’s workforce continues to expand. How can smaller school districts maintain robust educational programs and meet the talent needs of the Massachusetts economy? Our new study, Fewer Students, Greater Demand:...

The post Fewer Students, Greater Demand: How Regionalization Can Strengthen Massachusetts Schools appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] LDs 229 + 1089: Higher Taxes. Slower Growth. Fewer Jobs.
item.description:
Politicians in Augusta look poised to pass LDs 229 and 1089 this session, which would raise Maine’s top income tax rate to 10.95%. However, our research concludes that either one of these bills could individually cause serious damage to Maine’s economy if passed. If both bills were passed, it would stack multiple tax increases on […]

The post LDs 229 + 1089: Higher Taxes. Slower Growth. Fewer Jobs. appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Levy Caps for Property Tax Reform in Florida
item.description:
Introduction Florida’s current property tax system places a growing financial burden on homeowners, renters, and employers across the...

The post Levy Caps for Property Tax Reform in Florida appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] New Jersey Policy Institute Releases First “Pork Roll Report” as Governor Proposes FY2027 Budget
item.description:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE March 10, 2026 Trenton, NJ — As Governor Mikie Sherrill today proposed a $60.7 billion budget for Fiscal […]

The post New Jersey Policy Institute Releases First “Pork Roll Report” as Governor Proposes FY2027 Budget first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH could be the first state to let non-union public employees negotiate their contracts
item.description:
New Hampshire led the American colonies 250 years ago by adopt a constitution that created an independent government before anyone else. So it’s fitting that in 2026 New Hampshire could achieve another first for independence. Like all Americans, government employees have the constitutional right to free speech and free association. In 2018, the U.S. Supreme […]

The post NH could be the first state to let non-union public employees negotiate their contracts appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Report Overview: Creating Opportunities to Bring Seawater Desalination to Coastal Federal Lands in California
item.description:
Executive Summary California’s water supply challenges increasingly pose risks not only to the state’s economy, but to national food production, interstate commerce, international trade, and national economic stability and security. While seawater desalination is a proven technology used worldwide to expand reliable water supplies, large-scale projects along California’s coast have faced significant regulatory barriers at...

The post Report Overview: Creating Opportunities to Bring Seawater Desalination to Coastal Federal Lands in California first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Mackinac] Michigan lawmakers move to protect reasonable childhood independence
item.description: Trust parents and let kids grow

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Is Proof of Citizenship Really Jim Crow 2.0?
item.description:
As Major League Baseball prepares to open its 2026 season later this month, the return of baseball also serves as a reminder of how heated the election law debate became just a few years ago. In 2021, critics used the same “Jim Crow 2.0” label to describe Georgia’s election reform law, the Election Integrity Act...

The post Is Proof of Citizenship Really Jim Crow 2.0? first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Omaha Chamber CEO Heath Mello on Growing Nebraska
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome President and CEO of the Greater Omaha Chamber of Commerce, Heath Mello. During the episode Heath and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss how Nebraska can change course on migration trends, and how things like workforce, housing, and childcare will make a difference in growing...

The post Nebraskanomics: Omaha Chamber CEO Heath Mello on Growing Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] What History Tells Us About Trump’s Plan To Defeat Iran By Air
item.description:
President Donald Trump called for Iran’s “unconditional surrender” on March 6. But can he do that with U.S. air power alone? The answer is likely yes — if the Iranian people themselves are also the “boots on the ground.” Air power advocates have promised decisive results since the Italian general Giulio Douhet authored “The Command...

The post What History Tells Us About Trump’s Plan To Defeat Iran By Air first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Constitutional legitimacy: A final reason for a Montana constitutional convention
item.description: It is now far too late to challenge the constitution’s legality. However, Montanans deserve a constitution that is not under a legal cloud. A state constitutional convention could propose such a document.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] The Legislature shouldn’t decide what vaccines to make illegal
item.description:
A bill currently making its way through the Idaho Legislature could put the health of countless people at risk. Idaho’s Senate Health Committee recently atdvanced legislation that would impose a two-year moratorium on certain human gene therapy products and ban the use of mRNA vaccines for children and pregnant women for two years. If enacted, […]

The post The Legislature shouldn’t decide what vaccines to make illegal appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Why Data Centers Will Create Electricity Abundance
item.description:
There is concern that the energy requirements of data centers will consume so much electricity that demand will overwhelm supply. While this is certainly a possible outcome, the actual impact may have the opposite effect. For starters, while the total consumption of electricity by data centers is significant and growing, credible estimates point to manageable...

The post Why Data Centers Will Create Electricity Abundance first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Unleashing Students’ Potential: Georgia Legislators Champion Advanced Math Opportunities
item.description:
Georgia lawmakers have drafted a pair of bills that are set to transform the way math is taught in the state. If the bills become law, they’ll open doors for students to receive a high-quality math education and ultimately shape the direction of their lives.

The post Unleashing Students’ Potential: Georgia Legislators Champion Advanced Math Opportunities appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Haven’t We Been Here Before?
item.description: Last week, the U.S. House Committee on Energy and Commerce advanced several bills, three of which were related to youth online safety. The bills in question take a kitchen sink approach and combine previously considered social media and internet regulatory measures together. While the nuts and bolts differ between pieces of legislation, a trend is...

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Third-Grade Retention Will Not Recreate Billy Madison in Missouri
item.description:
In Jefferson City, there have been questions about the balance between academic promotion and social promotion in K–12 schools. In particular, there have been concerns about the effects a third-grade […]

The post Third-Grade Retention Will Not Recreate Billy Madison in Missouri appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas leads the world in ACT score declines
item.description: The Kansas Department of Education’s mantra – Kansas leads the world in the success of every student – would be more accurately stated as Kansas leads the world in ACT score declines. District-level ACT scores for 2025 were finally made public yesterday, and it’s no surprise that KSDE kept them hidden for months. The state […]

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: The Pampered Civic Mind Chesterton’s Fence: A principle advising against removing any law, institution or custom unless you first understand why it was put in place. A HANDFUL OF STUDENTS at a local high school organized a protest this week against imagined activity of Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents (there hasn’t been much). The students carried signs quoting Read the full article...

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Rooms for improvement: single-room occupancy in your state
item.description:
Rooms for improvement: single-room occupancy in your state By D. Dowd Muska  |  March 12, 2026 “I would never want to live like that, so therefore [insert housing type] should be illegal.” Online chatter generates intense heat, and measly light, but occasionally, a profound truth is posted. The quote above appeared on Reddit last year. […]

The post Rooms for improvement: single-room occupancy in your state appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Mackinac] Limit zoning to make Michigan grow again
item.description: Lawmakers should support housing reform

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Louisiana Supreme Court Delivers a Win for Common Sense and Economic Growth
item.description: What if a single court decision could unlock billions in investment, protect local decision-making, and secure Louisiana’s role as an energy powerhouse? That is exactly what happened when the Louisiana Supreme Court overturned an appellate ruling and affirmed St. James Parish’s authority to approve Koch Methanol’s plant expansion. This victory is not just a legal milestone. It...

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG: Public School Enrollment Plummets, Forcing Tough District Decisions
item.description:
IRG: Public School Enrollment Plummets, Forcing Tough District Decisions Declining enrollment darkens otherwise brightening school data Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) analyzed recent school data produced by the Department of Public Instruction. DPI’s data include performance indicators like attendance, suspensions, graduation, and college enrollment for the 2024-2025 school year as well […]

The post IRG: Public School Enrollment Plummets, Forcing Tough District Decisions appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] It’s Time to Phase Out the Earnings Tax. Honestly, Nothing Else Has Worked . . .
item.description:
A version of the following commentary appeared in the St. Louis Post-Dispatch. They say that the best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago, and the second-best time is […]

The post It’s Time to Phase Out the Earnings Tax. Honestly, Nothing Else Has Worked . . . appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] The continued protection of the use of taxpayer resources by government unions is baffling
item.description: Transparency is high on the list of every politician’s priorities. They should follow them instead of opting to promote a more opaque government that gives unions a massive gift in the form of easy fundraising.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Gov. Walz backs Senate DFL plan for $40 million cash handout to illegal immigrants
item.description:
The two parties in St. Paul have very different priorities this session. For Republicans, the focus is “affordability;” for the DFL, it is gun control and immigration. Part of the DFL’s immigration package is handout of $40 million of Minnesota taxpayers money to pay people’s rent. Fox 9 reports: Lawmakers say the state has seen...

The post Gov. Walz backs Senate DFL plan for $40 million cash handout to illegal immigrants appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] AI: When the Diary Talks Back
item.description:
Is this the newest form of indoctrination shaping the minds of our kids? Some people say letting children chat with AI is no different than letting them write in a diary. It’s private. It’s reflective. It’s simply a place to sort out thoughts on a page. But a diary doesn’t talk back. In “Harry Potter...

The post AI: When the Diary Talks Back first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Arizona’s Honors Colleges Hijacked by Activist Faculty to Force DEI on Students
item.description:
At Arizona State University’s Barrett Honors College, instructors for a mandatory course assign readings on “Compulsory Heterosexuality and Lesbian Experience”..

The post Arizona’s Honors Colleges Hijacked by Activist Faculty to Force DEI on Students appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Rochester police involved in fatal officer involved shooting with mentally ill man
item.description:
On Wednesday evening March 11th, 2026, at about 9:30 pm, Rochester Police fatally shot a man who reportedly grabbed an officer’s gun while being detained for a mental health hold. The fatal shooting was the first involving the Rochester Police Department since 2007, and it occurred despite a co-response by a mental health professional from...

The post Rochester police involved in fatal officer involved shooting with mentally ill man appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Economically, Feeling Better Isn’t the Same as Being Better
item.description:
Listen to this article In a series of sketches for Saturday Night Live, Billy Crystal played a fictionalized version of actor and director Fernando Lamas as host of the talk […]

The post Economically, Feeling Better Isn’t the Same as Being Better appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Iowa’s Next Budget Must Include Careful Spending Decisions
item.description:
Spending discipline will be critical as lawmakers build the FY2027 budget. New revenue estimates show modest slowing in revenue growth for the current year, reinforcing the need for careful budgeting as policymakers navigate economic uncertainty. Iowa faces new fiscal pressures despite economic positives. Slower revenue growth, federal policy changes, agricultural softness, and national economic uncertainty are creating a challenging budget environment, even as business investment, consumer spending, and employment remain strong. Strong reserves and AAA credit ratings provide stability—but discipline must continue. Iowa holds about $5.6 billion in reserves and recently had its AAA credit rating reaffirmed by Moody’s and Fitch, though both agencies warn that rising spending, particularly in Medicaid and education, could erode the state’s fiscal strength.

The post Iowa’s Next Budget Must Include Careful Spending Decisions appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Data center reassurances don’t stand a chance against ‘Terminator’
item.description:
The most recent Marquette Law School poll shows public opinion turning against data centers.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Has the U.S. Department of Education improved anything?
item.description: The agency has cost taxpayers more than $2.3 trillion. Yet despite this enormous investment, student performance has shown little meaningful improvement.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] War on Iran heightens calls for Jones Act relief
item.description:
America’s recent attacks on Iran and its immediate effect on fuel prices have spiked calls for the repeal or suspension of the Jones Act, the protectionist maritime law that a 2020 Grassroot Institute of Hawaii policy brief estimated costs Hawaii $1.2 billion a year. Some of the most recent headlines include “Trump Considers Waiving Jones […]

The post War on Iran heightens calls for Jones Act relief appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Senate Bill 1383 — Approp, veterans srvs, add’l (0)
item.description: Note: This year IFF rated maintenance bills according to a more refined system. This is an enhancement bill, and will be rated as a standalone bill. IFF will only consider enhancement line items in these ratings. This means that FTP reductions passed in maintenance legislation will not be evaluated here, among other things. Bill Description: Senate […]

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Let people live as they want, with or without cars

item.title: [Johnlocke] Justice Department takes on ‘corrupt’ DC Bar Association
item.description:
Ben Weingarten writes for the Federalist about a recent US Justice Department action. On Tuesday, word came that the legal disciplinary authority in Washington, D.C., was charging U.S. Pardon Attorney Ed Martin with ethics violations, kicking off proceedings that could result in penalties up to and including disbarment. In so doing, it might have just...

item.title: [Johnlocke] Ex-Starbucks CEO drops Seattle for Miami over taxes
item.description:
Taylor Herzlich writes for the New York Post about one executive’s recent move from a high-tax jurisdiction. Former Starbucks owner and CEO Howard Schultz says he is leaving Seattle for Miami after 44 years in the java giant’s home state of Washington — as Dems there have been pushing for a so-called “millionaires’ tax.” He...

The post Ex-Starbucks CEO drops Seattle for Miami over taxes appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] How Trump can make homeownership affordable
item.description: Michigan shows the wisdom of reforming state and local zoning laws

item.title: [Itrfoundation] National Conservatism Preserves What Freedom Alone Cannot
item.description:
National conservatism argues that conservatism must prioritize the nation’s common good, restoring sovereignty, moral order, and national cohesion after decades of emphasizing individual autonomy above all else. It contends that markets alone cannot preserve communities or national strength, and that prudent policies — including protective tariffs, immigration limits, and restrained foreign policy — can serve constitutional government and long-term stability. Rooted in tradition, moral order, and the Old Right tradition, national conservatism seeks to conserve what is enduring — America’s constitutional system, cultural inheritance, and national identity — while applying prudence to modern challenges.

The post National Conservatism Preserves What Freedom Alone Cannot appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Liberty First: Why Freedom Conservatism Beats National Conservatism Every Time
item.description:
Populism reflects real frustration with cultural decline and economic instability — but the solution matters. The key divide is whether populism empowers people or expands government power. National conservatism risks centralizing authority, using executive power, tariffs, and industrial policy in ways that blur constitutional limits and shift decision-making from citizens to politicians. Freedom conservatism argues that liberty, limited government, and separation of powers remain essential — trusting free people, not centralized power, as the surest path to prosperity, morality, and long-term stability.

The post Liberty First: Why Freedom Conservatism Beats National Conservatism Every Time appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Varvel: John Dewey
item.description: by GARY VARVEL  Proverbs 23:7 says, “For as he thinks in his heart, so is he.” How do you “fundamentally transform” a nation? Answer: Through education. As the saying goes, “The philosophy of the schoolroom in one generation will become the philosophy of the government in the next.” Transforming the next generation became the mission Read the full article...

item.title: [Johnlocke] And what about that federal Covid money for our schools?
item.description:
Three years ago, the federal government gave U.S. public schools $190 billion over five years to safeguard against Covid and reverse the academic fallout that followed. Was the money spent wisely? Were students better off because of the money? Were the goals of the programs accomplished? Those are questions investigators from the education web site The 74 Million...

The post And what about that federal Covid money for our schools? appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Even Maine’s Democratic Attorney General Argues Ranked-Choice Voting is Unconstitutional
item.description:
Last month, in our Open Letter to Maine’s Legislature, we urged lawmakers to stop advancing LD 1666, the bill that would force ranked-choice voting (RCV) on elections for Governor, State Senator, and State Representative. We reminded lawmakers of the Maine Constitution’s clear plurality requirement, the Maine Supreme Judicial Court’s unanimous 2017 advisory opinion declaring RCV […]

The post Even Maine’s Democratic Attorney General Argues Ranked-Choice Voting is Unconstitutional appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

Louisiana possesses the natural resources and geographic positioning to lead the nation,...

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 859 — Parenting time, restoration (+2)
item.description: Bill Description: House Bill 859 would add a provision to Chapter 7, Title 32, Idaho Code, which would require courts to restore a parent’s lost or restricted time with a child or children when allegations of abuse are unsubstantiated or not proven by clear and convincing evidence. Additionally, it strengthens enforcement remedies if there is […]

item.title: [Americanexperiment] How should technology fit in the classroom?
item.description:
While there are myriad harms associated with allowing students to access their personal digital devices in the classroom, the use of digital learning technology in the classroom might be a different matter entirely. A new paper by researcher Dr. Meredith Coffey argues for three tenets that should guide schools as they structure their digital landscape....

The post How should technology fit in the classroom? appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Senate Bill 1374 — Idaho child care program (-6)
item.description: Bill Description: Senate Bill 1374 would establish a state-administered child-care program, including eligibility rules, provider requirements, oversight, waiting lists, and program administration. Analyst Note: Senate Bill 1374 codifies a program that already exists in rules. In other words, the program was started without legislative approval, and this bill retroactively seeks approval to lawfully establish the […]

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Justice for the Tatoo-less IF YOU ARE TRYING to catch up with the times, you may be having trouble determining when it is proper to discriminate.
